## Execution Script

`~/.claude/skills/_youtube-execution/multicam_split.py`

---

## CRITICAL RULES (NEVER BREAK THESE)

### 1. NEVER Crop Out Faces
- Before final export, ALWAYS extract a preview frame and visually verify both faces are fully visible
- Use the TikTok safe zone overlay (`~/.claude/skills/multicam-split/assets/tiktok_safezone_1080x1920.png`) to check face placement on 9:16 exports
- The safe zone overlay is a REFERENCE ONLY — never bake it into the final render
- If faces are cropped, adjust `--left-crop-y` / `--right-crop-y` until both faces are fully in frame
- Faces must stay within the green safe zone rectangle (away from top/bottom bars and right-side TikTok UI)

### 2. ALWAYS Ask About Audio
- Every video file has its own audio track
- ALWAYS ask the user: "Which audio track should I use? And should I silence the others?"
- Never assume — the horizontal camera usually has the best audio, but the user decides
- Common scenarios:
  - Use horizontal audio only (silence verticals) — most common
  - Mix all 3 tracks (rare, for room ambience)
  - Use one vertical's audio (if it has the lav mic)

### 3. Safe Zone Verification (9:16 exports only)
- After compositing, extract a frame and overlay the TikTok safe zone template
- Verify faces and key content sit inside the safe rectangle
- The safe zone accounts for: top status bar, bottom caption area, right-side buttons (like/comment/share)
- Show the user the safe zone check frame before finalizing

### 4. ALWAYS Communicate Visually
- Video editing is visual work — NEVER describe crop positions, layouts, or adjustments with just numbers or text
- ALWAYS extract preview frames and show them as screenshots when:
  - Presenting layout options
  - Showing crop offset choices
  - Verifying face placement
  - Comparing before/after changes
- When presenting multiple options, save labeled screenshots to Desktop and open them in Preview
- **Annotated screenshots are even better** — use ffmpeg drawtext/drawbox overlays or Pillow to add:
  - Arrows pointing to what changed
  - Labels like "OPTION 1", "OPTION 2"
  - Highlight boxes around faces or key areas
  - Brief text explaining the difference
- The user should NEVER have to imagine what a change looks like — always show it

### 5. ALWAYS Open Files for the User
- When delivering exported files, ALWAYS run `open /path/to/file` so they pop up on screen immediately
- Do this for: exported videos, preview screenshots, safe zone checks — any file the user needs to see
- Also list the file paths in your response so the user knows where they are on disk
- Never just say "here's the file" without opening it — the user shouldn't have to go find it

### 6. File Naming Convention
- Extract **Title**, **Client**, and **Camera Code** from the original raw filename
- Original raw filenames follow this pattern: `{Title} - {Client} - {Description} - {CameraCode}_V{n}-{seq}`
  - Example: `Adding music to chores - Baileys - Chief Vibe Officer - C0055-001_V1-0105`
  - Title = `Adding music to chores`
  - Client = `Baileys`
  - Camera Code = `C0055`
- Format: `{Stage} - {Title} - {Client} - {CameraCode}.mov/.mp4`
- **Stages:**
  - `Raw` — composited split screen, unclipped, full length
  - `Unsubbed` — clipped/edited but no subtitles or overlays
  - `V1` — final polished with subtitles, images, overlays
  - `V2`, `V3`, etc. — split test variations (different hooks, lengths, etc.)
- **Examples:**
  - `Raw - Adding music to chores - Baileys - C0055.mov`
  - `Unsubbed - Adding music to chores - Baileys - C0055.mp4`
  - `V1 - Adding music to chores - Baileys - C0055.mp4`
  - `V2 - Adding music to chores - Baileys - C0055.mp4` (alt hook)
- Always ask the user to confirm the title, client, and camera code if the filename pattern is unclear

## TikTok Safe Zone Reference

Safe zone overlay is stored at:
`~/.claude/skills/multicam-split/assets/tiktok_safezone_1080x1920.png`

**Danger zones (where TikTok UI covers content):**
- Top 150px — status bar
- Bottom 270px — caption/description text
- Right 100px column (bottom half) — like, comment, share, save buttons
- Bottom-left 700x150px — username/handle area

**Safe rectangle for faces and key content:**
- Left: 30px
- Top: 150px
- Right: 950px (1080 - 130)
- Bottom: 1650px (1920 - 270)

To manually check safe zone on any frame:
```bash
# Overlay safe zone on a preview frame (for checking only, never in final export)
ffmpeg -y -i preview_frame.jpg -i ~/.claude/skills/multicam-split/assets/tiktok_safezone_1080x1920.png \
    -filter_complex "[0][1]overlay=0:0" safezone_check.jpg
```
